Cupertino: iOS-styled widgets. Companion to the Material library, for apps that want to look at home on iOS / macOS. The set is intentionally small: CupertinoApp, CupertinoNavigationBar, CupertinoButton. Colors come from a separate CupertinoTheme rather than the Material ThemeData, and the active theme lives in the cupertinoCurrent var.

Imports

../foundation/widget, ../foundation/key, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/color, ../rendering/decoration, ../rendering/text, ../widgets/basic, ../gestures/detector

Types

CupertinoApp = ref object of StatelessWidget
  home*: Widget
  theme*: CupertinoTheme
Top-level Cupertino app widget. Equivalent of MaterialApp for iOS-styled UIs. 
CupertinoButton = ref object of StatelessWidget
  onPressed*: TapCallback
  child*: Widget
  filled*: bool
iOS-style button. Two flavors: plain (just a tappable label in the tint color) and filled (a rounded rectangle in the tint color with contrast text). 
CupertinoColors = object
  activeBlue*: Color         ## iOS system blue (tint for buttons, links).
  systemBackground*: Color   ## page background.
  label*: Color              ## primary text color.
  secondaryLabel*: Color     ## secondary text color (captions, hints).
  separator*: Color          ## hairline separator color.
A small set of system colors that mirror iOS's UIKit semantic colors. Used to build the active CupertinoTheme. 
CupertinoNavigationBar = ref object of StatelessWidget
  middle*: Widget
  leading*: Widget
  trailing*: Widget
A 44pt-tall iOS-style navigation bar with three slots: a leading widget on the left (commonly a back button), a centered title (middle), and a trailing widget on the right (commonly a done button). The background is the system background with 0.92 alpha to suggest the translucent effect iOS uses. 
CupertinoTheme = object
  brightness*: int           ## 0 = light, 1 = dark.
  colors*: CupertinoColors   ## the active palette.
  primaryColor*: Color       ## tint color (usually `colors.activeBlue`).
  fontFamily*: string        ## default font family.
Ambient theme for Cupertino widgets. Set the active theme via cupertinoCurrent = ... or let a CupertinoApp do it for you.

Vars

cupertinoCurrent: CupertinoTheme = cupertinoTheme(false, "SF Pro")
The ambient Cupertino theme. Read by every Cupertino widget during its build. CupertinoApp assigns to this var as part of its own build.

Consts

cupertinoDark = (activeBlue: (value: 4278879487'u),
                 systemBackground: (value: 4278190080'u),
                 label: (value: 4294967295'u),
                 secondaryLabel: (value: 4293651445'u),
                 separator: (value: 4281874490'u))
cupertinoLight = (activeBlue: (value: 4278221567'u),
                  systemBackground: (value: 4294967295'u),
                  label: (value: 4278190080'u),
                  secondaryLabel: (value: 4282137667'u),
                  separator: (value: 4291217096'u))

Procs

proc cupertinoApp(home: Widget; theme = cupertinoTheme(); key: Key = nil): CupertinoApp {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a CupertinoApp.

Inputs:

  • home: root widget. Required.
  • theme: CupertinoTheme to install. Defaults to light.
  • key: optional reconciliation key.

Effect: assigns theme to cupertinoCurrent so descendants pick it up, then paints the system background behind home.

proc cupertinoButton(child: Widget; onPressed: TapCallback = nil;
                     filled = false; key: Key = nil): CupertinoButton {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a CupertinoButton.

Inputs:

  • child: button label. Required.
  • onPressed: tap callback.
  • filled: if true, render as a filled rounded rectangle (8px radius). If false (default), render as a plain text-style tappable label.
  • key: optional reconciliation key.

Effect: paints either a tint-filled pill or just padded text, both tappable when onPressed is provided.

proc cupertinoNavigationBar(middle: Widget; leading: Widget = nil;
                            trailing: Widget = nil; key: Key = nil): CupertinoNavigationBar {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a CupertinoNavigationBar.

Inputs:

  • middle: centered widget (title). Required.
  • leading: optional leading widget (back button).
  • trailing: optional trailing widget (action button).
  • key: optional reconciliation key.

Effect: lays out as [leading?, expanded(center(middle)), trailing?] inside a 44pt-tall bar with translucent system background.

proc cupertinoTheme(dark = false; fontFamily = "SF Pro"): CupertinoTheme {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a CupertinoTheme.

Inputs:

  • dark: when true, returns a dark-mode theme using cupertinoDark colors. Default false (light).
  • fontFamily: default font name for Cupertino widgets. Default "SF Pro".

Output: a populated CupertinoTheme ready to assign to cupertinoCurrent or pass to cupertinoApp(theme = ...).

Methods

method build(w: CupertinoApp; ctx: BuildContext): Widget {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
method build(w: CupertinoButton; ctx: BuildContext): Widget {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
When filled, builds a tint-colored rounded (8px) rectangle with the child wrapped in edgeInsetsSymmetric(20, 10). When not filled, just wraps the child in edgeInsetsSymmetric(16, 8) padding. Wrapped in a GestureDetector if onPressed is set. 
method build(w: CupertinoNavigationBar; ctx: BuildContext): Widget {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

    forbids: [].}
method widgetTypeName(w: CupertinoNavigationBar): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
